project: Hal Cloud Daytona Sandbox Orchestration v1
description: Deliver a production-safe v1 orchestration layer for running hal workloads in Daytona sandboxes with terminal-only operations, atomic claims, stale-attempt recovery, idempotent PR side effects, secure auth profile handling, and Postgres/Turso storage parity.

userStories.title: [Schema] Create run state model
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want a persisted run state model so orchestration can track each submitted workload safely.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a runs table with columns: id, repo, base_branch, engine, auth_profile_id, status, retry_count, max_retries, cancel_requested_at, created_at, updated_at.
Restrict runs.status to exactly queued, claimed, running, retrying, succeeded, failed, and canceled.
Migration up and down paths succeed in CI for both Postgres and Turso configurations.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Schema] Create attempt and heartbeat model
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want attempt-level records so each retry and heartbeat can be audited and recovered correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Add an attempts table with columns: run_id, attempt_number, state, worker_id, lease_expires_at, last_heartbeat_at, sandbox_id, started_at, finished_at, failure_code.
Enforce one active attempt per run with a schema constraint, and a test verifies a second active claim is rejected.
Create indexes on attempts.run_id, attempts.state, and attempts.lease_expires_at.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Schema] Create auth profile and compatibility model
userStories.description: As an operator, I want reusable auth profiles so subscription credentials can be linked once and reused safely.
acceptanceCriteria: Add an auth_profiles table with columns: id, name, provider, secret_ref, compatibility_metadata, created_at, updated_at, revoked_at.
Enforce unique profile names per provider using a unique constraint on (provider, name).
Store compatibility_metadata fields required for worker compatibility checks: OS, architecture, and CLI version.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Schema] Create profile lock model
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want lease-based profile locks so concurrent runs cannot corrupt shared subscription sessions.
acceptanceCriteria: Add an auth_profile_locks table with columns: profile_id, run_id, worker_id, lease_expires_at, last_heartbeat_at, created_at.
Enforce at most one active lock per profile.
Add an index on lock lease expiry to support stale-lock reconciliation.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Schema] Create idempotency and event log model
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want durable side-effect and event records so retries remain safe and observable.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a run_side_effects table keyed by (run_id, effect_type, idempotency_key).
Add an append-only run_events table with columns: run_id, attempt_number, event_type, message, redacted, created_at.
Add a uniqueness rule that prevents duplicate pr_create side effects for the same run and idempotency key.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement storage contract and Postgres adapter
userStories.description: As a backend engineer, I want a single orchestration storage interface with a Postgres implementation so business logic stays database-agnostic.
acceptanceCriteria: Define storage interface methods for run create, claim, update, attempts, events, side effects, and profile locks.
Implement a Postgres adapter for all methods in the storage interface.
Adapter integration tests cover create, claim, heartbeat, retry transition, cancel, and side-effect upsert paths.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement Turso adapter with parity test suite
userStories.description: As a backend engineer, I want a Turso implementation that matches Postgres behavior so v1 can run on either backend.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement a Turso adapter for the same storage interface used by Postgres.
Run one shared adapter test suite against both adapters with identical expected outcomes.
Normalize duplicate-key and timeout errors into shared domain error types.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement submit validation and run creation
userStories.description: As an operator, I want invalid runs rejected at submit time so workers only process compatible, policy-allowed jobs.
acceptanceCriteria: Submit validates required fields: repo, base branch, engine, and auth profile.
Submit fails fast when engine and profile provider are incompatible or when the profile is revoked.
Successful submit writes a runs row in queued state and inserts the first run_events entry.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement atomic claim and lease acquisition
userStories.description: As a worker, I want run claiming to be atomic so multiple workers never process the same run at once.
acceptanceCriteria: Claim transitions exactly one queued or retrying run to claimed within one transaction.
Claim creates an attempt record and acquires the profile lock in the same transaction, or rolls back all changes on failure.
A concurrency test with parallel workers shows one winner per run and zero duplicate claims.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement heartbeat renewal for attempts and profile locks
userStories.description: As a worker, I want periodic lease renewal so healthy attempts are not reclaimed prematurely.
acceptanceCriteria: Worker heartbeat updates attempts.last_heartbeat_at and auth_profile_locks.last_heartbeat_at for the active run.
Heartbeat extends lease_expires_at for both the attempt lease and profile lock lease.
If lease is missing or expired, worker stops processing and emits a terminal event.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement stale reconciler and retry scheduling
userStories.description: As an operator, I want stale attempts recovered automatically so transient failures do not require manual intervention.
acceptanceCriteria: Reconciler scans expired leases on a fixed interval and marks stale attempts as failed.
Eligible runs transition to retrying and then return to queued with incremented retry_count.
Integration test demonstrates stale recovery from expired lease to retry-ready queued state in under 30 seconds p95.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement Daytona sandbox bootstrap pipeline
userStories.description: As a worker, I want deterministic sandbox setup so runs start consistently and fail with actionable error codes when setup breaks.
acceptanceCriteria: Worker provisions a Daytona sandbox, persists sandbox_id, and records sandbox lifecycle events.
Bootstrap performs repository clone, branch checkout, and runs hal init only when .hal is missing.
Bootstrap failure classification maps errors to retryable and non-retryable failure codes.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement auth profile materialization and preflight checks
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want headless-safe auth handling so workers can use subscription credentials without interactive login.
acceptanceCriteria: Worker resolves secret_ref and materializes provider auth files with restrictive file permissions.
Worker runs provider-specific non-interactive preflight checks before executing hal.
Worker rejects incompatible profiles when stored compatibility metadata does not match OS, architecture, or CLI version.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement secret redaction for logs/events
userStories.description: As an operator, I want sensitive output redacted so operational logs remain safe to store and stream.
acceptanceCriteria: Redaction filters mask tokens, device codes, cookies, and secret-like patterns before persistence and streaming.
Redaction runs on bootstrap logs, preflight logs, runtime logs, and error messages.
Test fixtures with known secrets verify persisted and streamed logs contain [REDACTED] and never raw secret values.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement idempotent PR side-effect executor
userStories.description: As an operator, I want retries to reuse prior PR side effects so duplicate PRs/comments are never created.
acceptanceCriteria: PR create, update, and comment operations require deterministic idempotency keys.
Side-effect handler returns a previously stored PR reference when the same key already exists.
Retry simulation confirms exactly one PR create external call across repeated attempt failures.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement cancel flow and worker shutdown handling
userStories.description: As an operator, I want cancel requests to stop active work quickly and safely.
acceptanceCriteria: Cancel API marks run cancellation as requested and appends a cancel event.
Active worker detects cancellation within one heartbeat interval, stops execution, and tears down the sandbox.
Canceled runs are not eligible for future claims or retries.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Frontend] Add hal-cloud submit command UX
userStories.description: As an operator, I want a simple submit command so I can enqueue a run from terminal with clear feedback.
acceptanceCriteria: Command supports --repo, --base, --engine, --auth-profile, and task or PRD reference input.
On success, command prints run ID and initial status in human-readable format.
On validation error, command prints an actionable message and exits with a non-zero code.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using agent-browser skill (skip if no dev server running)

userStories.title: [Frontend] Add hal-cloud status command UX
userStories.description: As an operator, I want status output so I can inspect run state and retry health from terminal.
acceptanceCriteria: Command displays run status, current attempt, retry count, last heartbeat age, and profile name.
--json output returns stable machine-readable fields.
Unknown run ID returns a non-zero exit code with an explicit not-found message.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using agent-browser skill (skip if no dev server running)

userStories.title: [Frontend] Add hal-cloud logs command UX
userStories.description: As an operator, I want live logs so I can diagnose orchestration behavior without external tools.
acceptanceCriteria: logs <run-id> returns historical events in timestamp order.
logs <run-id> --follow streams newly appended events until interrupted.
Displayed logs preserve [REDACTED] masking for sensitive fields.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using agent-browser skill (skip if no dev server running)

userStories.title: [Frontend] Add hal-cloud cancel command UX
userStories.description: As an operator, I want a clear cancel command so I can stop an active run safely from terminal.
acceptanceCriteria: cancel <run-id> supports --yes and prints the final cancel outcome.
Canceling a completed run returns a no-op message with exit code 0.
Canceling an active run returns a pending-cancel state until worker confirmation is received.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using agent-browser skill (skip if no dev server running)

userStories.title: [Frontend] Add auth profile command UX
userStories.description: As an operator, I want terminal auth profile commands so I can manage provider credentials without leaving the CLI.
acceptanceCriteria: auth link --provider --profile, auth status, and auth validate commands are available and documented in help output.
auth status shows lock owner, lock expiry, and a compatibility metadata summary.
auth validate returns pass or fail and exits with a non-zero code on failure.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using agent-browser skill (skip if no dev server running)
